title: Cozy Dense Norwegian Fry-Grind-Rice-Khao-Khua Pilaf
description: A hearty and comforting Norwegian-inspired dish featuring blackcurrant jam and versatile rice-khao-khua.
instructions: Rinse 1 cup of rice-khao-khua in a fine-mesh strainer under cold water, then drain well.
In a large saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add 1 small onion, finely chopped, and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add 2 cloves of garlic, minced, and cook for 1 minute more.
Add 1 cup of rice-khao-khua to the saucepan and stir to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the water is absorbed and the rice-khao-khua is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in 2 tablespoons of blackcurrant jam.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
